meaning: n.化石,僵化的事物adj.化石的,陈腐的,守旧的
examples: Burning fossil fuels uses oxygen and produces carbon dioxide.
On a global scale, 77% of energy is created from fossil fuels.
The electricity industry consumes large amounts of fossil fuels.
Human sources come principally from the burning of fossil fuels such as coal, oil and gas.
Later in the program, trading snowmobiles for stegoceras bones--a look into the international fossil market.
